headline: Blood Test Identifies Alzheimer's Risk in Healthy People
meta_description: A new blood test can identify healthy people at high risk for Alzheimer's disease, enabling early intervention and monitoring.
summary_short: Scientists have developed a blood test that can identify people without symptoms who are at high risk for Alzheimer's disease, opening doors for early intervention.
body:
Researchers have developed a blood test capable of identifying people who are still healthy but at significant risk for Alzheimer's disease. This advance offers a powerful tool for early detection and intervention, potentially changing the trajectory of the disease for thousands of people.

Early identification allows people to make informed decisions about their health, explore preventive strategies, and work with doctors to monitor their cognitive health over time. The test represents a shift toward proactive medicine—catching disease risk before symptoms appear, when interventions can be most effective. This kind of advance in medical science gives people agency and hope in the face of a condition that has long seemed inevitable.
